philosophy: Grandeur, collective power, historical momentum. The visual language of Chinese state communications, epic national documentaries, and large-venue political theater. Scale conveys importance. Red is not decoration — it is civilization.
core_principles: Scale creates gravity — bigger is more important, unapologetically
Red and gold are semantic, not decorative — they carry civilizational weight
Center-focus is strength — decentralized compositions dilute authority
Motion is majestic, not playful — slow builds into powerful reveals
Collective before individual — the frame holds the nation, not the person
Typography carries tradition — serif for authority, sans for modernity
Contrast is dramatic — light/dark, large/small, fast/slow
forbidden_patterns: Western minimalist 'white space as luxury' aesthetic
Small type as design choice (authority requires legibility)
Fragmented layouts that lack clear focal center
Glass morphism (feels insubstantial for this tradition)
